name: Electronics and Communication Engineering
description: A comprehensive program covering electronic devices, circuits, communication systems, and signal processing. Prepares students for careers in telecommunications, embedded systems, VLSI, and related fields.

subjects.name: Basic Electronics
subjects.description: Fundamentals of semiconductor physics, PN junction diodes, bipolar junction transistors, and field-effect transistors. Covers rectifiers, amplifiers, and basic electronic circuits.
learning_outcomes: Analyze and design basic diode and transistor circuits
Understand semiconductor physics and charge carrier behavior
Build and test rectifier and amplifier circuits
Interpret device characteristics from datasheets
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for all electronics roles in consumer electronics, semiconductor, and telecommunications industries.

subjects.name: Electronic Devices
subjects.description: In-depth study of semiconductor devices including MOSFETs, BJTs, JFETs, and thyristors. Covers device physics, I-V characteristics, and small-signal modeling.
learning_outcomes: Model and analyze MOSFET and BJT characteristics
Design biasing circuits for various transistor configurations
Analyze frequency response of electronic devices
Evaluate device performance parameters from experimental data
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for VLSI design, semiconductor fabrication, and analog circuit design roles.

subjects.name: Digital Electronics
subjects.description: Covers Boolean algebra, combinational and sequential logic circuits, flip-flops, counters, registers, and logic families. Includes design of arithmetic circuits and finite state machines.
learning_outcomes: Design and optimize combinational logic circuits using Boolean algebra
Implement sequential circuits including counters and registers
Analyze and design finite state machines
Use HDL tools for digital circuit simulation
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for digital design, FPGA development, and ASIC design roles in semiconductor industry.

subjects.name: Analog Electronics
subjects.description: Design and analysis of analog circuits including operational amplifiers, feedback amplifiers, oscillators, and power amplifiers. Covers frequency response, stability, and compensation techniques.
learning_outcomes: Design operational amplifier circuits for various applications
Analyze feedback topologies and their effect on amplifier performance
Design sinusoidal and non-sinusoidal oscillators
Evaluate stability and compensation in multistage amplifiers
subjects.industry_relevance: Widely used in audio systems, sensor interfaces, power management ICs, and analog front-end design.

subjects.name: Signals and Systems
subjects.description: Fundamentals of continuous and discrete-time signals and systems. Covers Fourier series, Fourier transform, Laplace transform, Z-transform, convolution, and system analysis.
learning_outcomes: Analyze continuous and discrete-time signals using transforms
Characterize LTI systems in time and frequency domains
Apply convolution and correlation to signal processing problems
Design and analyze sampling and reconstruction systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Core mathematical foundation for communications, image processing, audio engineering, and control systems.

subjects.name: Network Theory
subjects.description: Analysis of electrical networks using mesh and nodal analysis, network theorems, two-port networks, and network functions. Covers transient and steady-state response.
learning_outcomes: Apply mesh and nodal analysis to complex electrical networks
Use network theorems to simplify circuit analysis
Characterize two-port networks using parameters
Analyze transient and steady-state response of RLC circuits
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for circuit design, power systems, and electronic system analysis.

subjects.name: Electromagnetic Theory
subjects.description: Study of static and time-varying electromagnetic fields, Maxwell's equations, wave propagation, transmission lines, and waveguides. Includes boundary conditions and plane wave reflection.
learning_outcomes: Solve boundary value problems in electrostatics and magnetostatics
Apply Maxwell's equations to analyze time-varying fields
Analyze wave propagation in different media
Design and characterize transmission line and waveguide structures
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for antenna design, microwave engineering, optical communication, and EMC compliance.

subjects.name: Communication Systems
subjects.description: Principles of analog communication including AM, FM, PM modulation techniques, transmitters, receivers, and noise analysis. Covers multiplexing, superheterodyne receivers, and communication link design.
learning_outcomes: Design analog modulation and demodulation systems
Analyze noise performance of analog communication systems
Design superheterodyne receiver architecture
Evaluate trade-offs in modulation schemes for given applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to broadcast radio, television, satellite communications, and aerospace telemetry.

subjects.name: Digital Communication
subjects.description: Digital modulation techniques, information theory, channel coding, spread spectrum, and multiple access schemes. Covers error control coding, line coding, and synchronization.
learning_outcomes: Design and analyze digital modulation schemes like PSK, QAM, FSK
Apply information theory concepts to source and channel coding
Implement error detection and correction codes
Evaluate performance of communication systems in noisy channels
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for 4G/5G cellular, Wi-Fi, satellite, and deep-space communication systems.

subjects.name: Microprocessors and Microcontrollers
subjects.description: Architecture, programming, and interfacing of microprocessors (8085/8086) and microcontrollers (8051/ARM). Covers instruction sets, memory interfacing, interrupts, and peripheral devices.
learning_outcomes: Program microprocessors and microcontrollers in assembly and C
Design memory and I/O interfacing circuits
Implement interrupt-driven and real-time applications
Develop embedded firmware for microcontroller-based systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Ubiquitous in automotive, consumer electronics, IoT devices, and industrial automation.

subjects.name: Embedded Systems
subjects.description: Design of embedded systems including RTOS, peripheral interfacing, sensor integration, and low-power design. Covers ARM Cortex-M architecture, communication protocols, and system-on-chip concepts.
learning_outcomes: Design embedded systems with ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers
Implement real-time operating system concepts for multitasking
Interface sensors, actuators, and communication peripherals
Optimize embedded software for power and memory constraints
subjects.industry_relevance: Powers virtually all modern smart devices including wearables, automotive ECUs, medical devices, and home automation.

subjects.name: VLSI Design
subjects.description: CMOS VLSI design covering digital and analog IC design, layout, fabrication, and design verification. Includes standard cell design, ASIC flow, and FPGA implementation.
learning_outcomes: Design CMOS digital and analog circuits at transistor level
Create physical layouts following design rules
Implement digital designs using HDL and synthesis tools
Analyze timing, power, and area trade-offs in VLSI design
subjects.industry_relevance: Core skill for semiconductor industry roles in companies like Intel, AMD, Qualcomm, and TSMC.

subjects.name: Digital Signal Processing
subjects.description: Theory and application of DSP including DFT, FFT, digital filter design, multirate processing, and adaptive filtering. Covers fixed-point implementation and real-time processing constraints.
learning_outcomes: Design FIR and IIR digital filters for given specifications
Implement DFT and FFT algorithms for spectral analysis
Apply multirate signal processing techniques
Implement adaptive filters for noise cancellation and channel equalization
subjects.industry_relevance: Used in audio/video processing, radar, sonar, biomedical signal analysis, and speech recognition systems.

subjects.name: Antennas and Wave Propagation
subjects.description: Antenna theory, types, parameters, and radio wave propagation mechanisms. Covers dipole, patch, horn, and array antennas along with propagation models for various frequency bands.
learning_outcomes: Design and analyze various antenna types for given specifications
Calculate antenna parameters including gain, directivity, and radiation pattern
Analyze radio wave propagation in different frequency bands
Design antenna arrays for beamforming applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for wireless communications, radar, satellite, aerospace, and IoT connectivity solutions.

subjects.name: Microwave Engineering
subjects.description: Microwave components, circuits, and measurement techniques including S-parameters, microwave amplifiers, oscillators, mixers, and filters. Covers microstrip and stripline technologies.
learning_outcomes: Design microwave components using S-parameter analysis
Design microstrip filters, couplers, and power dividers
Analyze microwave amplifier and oscillator circuits
Perform microwave measurements using network analyzers
subjects.industry_relevance: Core competency for 5G/mmWave systems, radar, satellite communication, and defense electronics.

subjects.name: Wireless Communication
subjects.description: Cellular concepts, wireless standards, fading channels, diversity techniques, MIMO systems, and OFDM. Covers evolution from 2G to 5G/6G including LTE, NR, and beyond.
learning_outcomes: Analyze cellular system design including frequency reuse and handoff
Design and evaluate MIMO and OFDM systems
Model wireless channels and apply diversity techniques
Understand 5G NR architecture and radio interface protocols
subjects.industry_relevance: Drives the mobile communications industry powering smartphones, IoT, and broadband wireless access.

subjects.name: Optical Communication
subjects.description: Fiber optic communication systems including optical fibers, sources, detectors, amplifiers, and WDM systems. Covers link design, dispersion management, and nonlinear effects.
learning_outcomes: Design fiber optic links considering attenuation and dispersion
Analyze performance of optical transmitters and receivers
Design WDM systems with optical amplifiers
Troubleshoot nonlinear effects in high-speed optical networks
subjects.industry_relevance: Backbone of the internet, data center interconnects, and long-haul telecommunications infrastructure.
